title: From physics-based intelligence to molecular design: linking mechanistic learning across scales
excerpt: This article explores the integration of physics-based computational approaches with artificial intelligence to advance molecular design by establishing mechanistic learning frameworks across multiple biological and chemical scales. The work, authored by researchers at Shanghai Jiao Tong University and affiliated institutions, addresses the connection between fundamental physical principles and practical drug discovery applications. The methodology aims to improve the rational design of pharmaceutical compounds by leveraging physics-informed machine learning to understand and predict molecular behavior.

title: Beyond one-size-fits-all: Latent patient classes at methadone treatment entry and their clinical implications - Evidence from a large real-world cohort
excerpt: This real-world cohort study identifies distinct patient subgroups at the point of methadone treatment initiation using latent class analysis, revealing heterogeneous clinical profiles that have implications for personalized treatment approaches. The findings challenge standardized one-size-fits-all treatment protocols and suggest that patient stratification at treatment entry may better inform clinical management strategies. The research provides evidence from a large cohort to support tailored interventions based on baseline patient characteristics in opioid use disorder treatment.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Protein Design: A retrospective study on 20-year emerging trends and core research areas from bibliometric perspectives
excerpt: This bibliometric analysis examines 20 years of research trends at the intersection of artificial intelligence and protein design, identifying core research areas and emerging developments in the field. The study systematically maps the evolution of scientific publications and research focus areas to provide a retrospective overview of how AI applications have shaped protein design research. This comprehensive analysis offers insights into the trajectory and current landscape of AI-driven protein design innovation.

title: Precision therapeutics for wound healing: Molecular targets, signalling networks, and advanced drug design strategies
excerpt: This review examines precision therapeutic approaches for wound healing, focusing on molecular targets and signalling networks that drive the healing process. The article outlines advanced drug design strategies that leverage these mechanistic insights to develop targeted interventions for wound repair. The work synthesizes current understanding of protein-level mechanisms relevant to therapeutic development in this field.

title: Small molecular therapeutic targets for neurodegenerative diseases
excerpt: This review examines small molecule therapeutic targets relevant to neurodegenerative diseases, drawing on current research in neurochemistry and biotechnology to identify molecular pathways and compounds of interest for drug development in this therapeutic area.

title: Recent strategies and approaches for the development of molecularly targeted nanomedicine
excerpt: This review examines current strategies and approaches for developing molecularly targeted nanomedicines, which represent an advancing field in drug delivery technology designed to improve therapeutic specificity and efficacy.

title: Augmenting Medical Judgment in Signal Confirmation: Design Considerations for an AI-Enabled Causality Assessment Framework
excerpt: This paper presents design considerations for an artificial intelligence-enabled framework intended to support medical judgment in causality assessment during drug safety signal confirmation. The framework is developed to augment—rather than replace—human expertise in evaluating potential adverse drug reactions. The work addresses practical implementation aspects for integrating AI tools into pharmacovigilance processes.

title: Evolution of KRAS molecular glues: from allele-selective to Pan-RAS and protein-protein interaction modulators
excerpt: This review examines the development of KRAS-targeted molecular glues, tracing their evolution from allele-selective inhibitors to pan-RAS inhibitors and protein-protein interaction modulators. The article documents advances in targeting strategies for KRAS mutations, a significant focus in oncology drug development. The work synthesizes current approaches to modulating KRAS function through distinct molecular mechanisms.

title: Primary tumor resection prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival in metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors: a systematic review and meta-analysis
excerpt: A systematic review and meta-analysis demonstrates that primary tumor resection performed prior to peptide receptor radionuclide therapy is associated with improved survival outcomes in patients with metastatic gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumors. This finding suggests a potential treatment sequencing benefit for this patient population. The analysis supports consideration of surgical resection as part of a multimodal treatment strategy before initiating peptide receptor radionuclide therapy.

title: FDA Issues Emergency Use Authorization for Drug for Short-Term Prevention of New World Screwworm in Horses
excerpt: The FDA today issued an Emergency Use Authorization (EUA) for Ivermectin Liquid for Horses (ivermectin oral solution) for the short-term prevention of New World screwworm (NWS) infestations (myiasis) in horses when administered within 24 hours of birth or at the time of initial wound care.

title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
excerpt: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution.
